title: English screens
wikitext:
[]: English screens             Quote Post by Barinax » Tue Aug 05, 2008 5:36 pm http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ryXs0fxq1eQ I think these were the ones that aired on Cartoon Network or something (Adult Swim?) a while back (maybe ten years?). Can I find this anywhere else? Have they been released? And... what do you think of them? Do you think they are accurate?

[]:             Quote Post by swamp147 » Tue Aug 05, 2008 10:22 pm Sorry this post doesn't really answer your questions. I just wanted to say how weird it was to watch NGE in English. I've always watched the English-subbed Japanese version, and watching it in English was different. I actually think I like it better, since I can focus on the scene more when I'm not reading the subtitles. Anyways, isn't that the ADV dub? If so, most scenes should be accurate; there are a couple that they changed (for the worse). Edit - eew I don't like Asuka's and Hikari's English voices. Rei and Shinji are fine though.

[]:             Quote Post by Vegeta 20XX » Wed Aug 06, 2008 3:58 am It's funny, I've always watched the English dubs - no Japanese and no subtitles (except when necessary).

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Aug 06, 2008 2:08 pm Episode 25, from ADV's perfect collection.

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelion__x » Sat Aug 09, 2008 1:56 am Vegeta 20XX wrote: It's funny, I've always watched the English dubs - no Japanese and no subtitles (except when necessary).

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sat Aug 09, 2008 6:12 am Subs are better. Dubs just completely wipes out the original voices, anbd many dubs doesn't make up for the original drama done by the originals. Dubs, may also prove annoying because they are trying to fit it to the mouth movement, ergo, may be incomplete, while subs will provide a better translation without restriction.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Sat Aug 09, 2008 11:10 am Dubs just completely wipes out the original voices,

[]:             Quote Post by Barinax » Sat Aug 09, 2008 2:26 pm Somethings don't translate as well, and you end reading what was meant to be heard. I like subs, but I don't think dubs are really that bad.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Aug 10, 2008 2:08 am I still insist that dubs will wipe out the original character (as in dramaticness) of the original, and somethings may end up lost in translation.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Sun Aug 10, 2008 11:56 am "Lost in translation?" Excuse me, if you are going to translate something, if its going to get lost in translation, its going to happen regardless if its a sub or a dub. Correct me if I'm wrong, but subs actually get mistranslated more frequently because the people who are translating are often Japanese trying to understand English (which is quite hard), rather than English speakers trying to understand Japanese (not as hard). If its English speakers trying to translate it, translation errors are more likely, but the English will be coherent because if something doesn't quite make sense, they just wing it and make something approximately right that is at least a coherent sentence. And even if it doesn't make a coherent sentence, the voice actor in a dub will catch it and change it into something that makes sense to them. Why? Because when you say something out loud, it just... well it just doesn't sound right. You know what I'm talking about, when you say something out loud in your native tounge, incoherent sentences are impossible to miss. The result is a smoother experience that sounds like it was written by a native speaker, even though it might have mistakes in translation. Overall, that makes for a better viewing experience, even if it isn't exactly like the original. Also, the character may be something that doesn't translate culturally anyway. But the actor can make it something that makes sense in their own culture, thereby making it easier for the audience to comprehend. Yes, this means that us normal people who haven't dedicated a significant portion of our lives to becoming a weeabo can appreciate the work. *gasp!* Take Crayon Shin Chan. I believe Action Bastard once pointed out that they didn't just change languages when they dubbed it: they went ahead and changed the dialogue outright so an American audience could appreciate the jokes, not just understand them. Otherwise, it wouldn't be entertaining. Isn't that part of the point of translating something? Making it availiable to a wider audience? If that audience needs to see something that has been translated culturally as well as language wise, thats a small price to pay. I've seen more dubs than I've seen subs, but I've also enjoyed more dubs than I've enjoyed subs. And that doesn't just go for anime. That goes for all translations I've seen.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Aug 10, 2008 12:13 pm Hofstadter, in his Metamagical Themas anthology, did an interesting bit about translation, which anyone interested in the subject of subbing and dubbing should read. The Italians have a neat phrase, "traditore, tradutore", for the problem. Formless One wrote: Also, the character may be something that doesn't translate culturally anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Barinax » Sun Aug 10, 2008 2:09 pm Background... hmm... I still think both sub and dub should be seen if possible (in my case). One episode became much more funnier when I heard it. The episode was in the form of a low budget movie, and with the Japanese, I couldn't tell if the acting was intentionally bad. With the sub I smiled, but with the dub I laughed out loud. There were a few voices I couldn't stand, though. Wait. Why... why are we talking about dubs? This doesn't have much to do with dubs.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Aug 10, 2008 11:33 pm Dubs are worse, mainly because they are trying to fit it within the time in wich the mouth is moving, so they need to do more adjustments then subs. From my experience, most subs are reasonably accurate (except some misusage of idioms, but that is not a big deal)

[]:             Quote Post by Barinax » Mon Aug 11, 2008 12:15 am I'm not Japanese so I might benefit from seeing both. Okay, that's enough about dubs in here. I never mentioned dubs in my post; someone brought it up.
